timestamp_capture: Word timestamps come from whisper_timestamped.transcribe(...).segments[].words[]. Each raw word start/end is relative to its MP3. Display tokens are reconciled to those raw words by normalized character matching; unmatched tokens are interpolated only when the unmatched count stays under the configured threshold.

cues.text: ED-ALPHA is an open benchmark for predicting future, investment-relevant corporate events.
This walkthrough shows how public news signals are turned into ranked predictions and evaluated against later SEC Form 8-K events.
The task is a forward-looking ranking problem; predict which companies will later file investment-relevant Form 8-K events within the evaluation horizon.
At the prediction date, the model can use only information already public, including SEC company metadata and recent news.
Each model ranks companies by how likely they are to file a relevant Form 8-K event.
Ground-truth labels come from SEC Form 8-K filings in the evaluation horizon, meaning the future window the model is trying to predict.
Finally, the dashboard evaluates the Top-K ranking with precision, recall, F1, and true-positive counts.
Start with the controls.
The experiment selector fixes the benchmark scenario.
Here, Experiment 20 defines the prediction date, horizon, sampling seed, and tracked event types.
The run selector chooses one scoring execution inside that same fixed task.
That lets a reviewer compare different models without changing the benchmark setup.
Next, read the experiment configuration.
The prediction date is August 31, 2025, and the event horizon is five days.
The seed and negative multiplier make the snapshot reproducible.
The item-code list states which Form 8-K events count as positives.
These details are what make a dashboard result traceable back to a fixed benchmark definition.
The metrics panel shows how ranking quality changes with K.
Precision tells us how many selected companies are correct.
Recall tells us how much of the future event set has been recovered.
F1 summarizes that balance, while true positives gives the concrete count.
The table turns the metric summary into concrete ranked companies.
Each row shows the company, model score, supporting news signal, outcome, and matched event.
A hit means the ranked company later filed a tracked event during the evaluation window.
The news summary explains why the model ranked this company before the event window.
The event column shows the later Form 8-K match.
The final step is export.
Researchers can keep the selected experiment and ranking results as a JSON snapshot.
Thank you for watching. For details on running ED-ALPHA and preparing run data, please visit the ED-ALPHA GitHub repository page.
